prompt: Add template inheritance support. A child template should be able to extend a base template and override specific blocks.

turns.content:
<task>
Add template inheritance support. A child template should be able to extend a base template and override specific blocks.
</task>

1: import re
2: from typing import Dict
3: 
4: class Template:
5:     def __init__(self, content: str):
6:         self.content = content
7: 
8:     def render(self, context: Dict[str, str]) -> str:
9:         result = self.content
10:         for key, value in context.items():
11:             result = result.replace('{{ ' + key + ' }}', value)
12:         return result

arguments.summary: Read src/template_engine.py (12 lines). Basic template with variable substitution. No block or inheritance support. Need to add {% block name %}...{% endblock %} and {% extends 'base' %} directives.

arguments.content:
from src.template_engine import TemplateEngine

def test_simple_render():
    engine = TemplateEngine()
    engine.register('hello', 'Hello {{ name }}!')
    assert engine.render('hello', {'name': 'Alice'}) == 'Hello Alice!'

def test_block_inheritance():
    engine = TemplateEngine()
    engine.register('base', '<html>{% block title %}Default{% endblock %}<body>{% block content %}{% endblock %}</body></html>')
    engine.register('page', "{% extends 'base' %}{% block title %}My Page{% endblock %}{% block content %}Hello World{% endblock %}")
    result = engine.render('page')
    assert '<html>My Page<body>Hello World</body></html>' == result

def test_partial_override():
    engine = TemplateEngine()
    engine.register('base', '{% block header %}DEFAULT HEADER{% endblock %} | {% block footer %}DEFAULT FOOTER{% endblock %}')
    engine.register('child', "{% extends 'base' %}{% block header %}CUSTOM HEADER{% endblock %}")
    result = engine.render('child')
    assert result == 'CUSTOM HEADER | DEFAULT FOOTER'

def test_standalone_with_blocks():
    engine = TemplateEngine()
    engine.register('standalone', 'Before {% block main %}content{% endblock %} After')
    assert engine.render('standalone') == 'Before content After'

arguments.summary: All 4 tests pass. Template inheritance works: child extends parent, overrides specific blocks, unoverridden blocks keep parent defaults.
arguments.final_answer: Added template inheritance to src/template_engine.py. TemplateEngine.register() stores templates by name. render() detects {% extends 'parent' %}, extracts child block overrides with regex, replaces matching parent blocks. Unoverridden blocks keep parent content. All 4 tests pass.
